name: Amazon Echo / Alexa
short_description: A voice-controlled smart speaker line driven by the Alexa cloud assistant.
long_description: The Amazon Echo is a far-field-listening home speaker that puts the Alexa voice assistant into the room as an always-listening domestic object. By answering to a wake word and controlling music, timers, shopping, and connected smart-home devices through speech alone, it normalized ambient, voice-first AI in everyday households and became the template for the smart-speaker category. It matters for physical AI because it shows how a minimally expressive appliance can act as the hub of an intelligent home without any face, screen, or moving parts.

images.caption: The first-generation Amazon Echo smart speaker.
images.alt_text: A tall black cylindrical Amazon Echo smart speaker standing upright.

sources.title: Amazon Echo
sources.access_notes: History, launch date (November 6, 2014), and product line details.

interactions.title: Chain a morning into one word
interactions.description: Say "Alexa, good morning" and a Routine fires off a sequence on its own — lights up, blinds open, the day's weather and commute read aloud, news starts playing — all stitched to a single phrase or a time of day.
interactions.source_label: Amazon — What you can do with Routines

interactions.title: Drop in on the kitchen from upstairs
interactions.description: Use Echoes around the house as an intercom: say "Alexa, drop in on the kitchen" and a two-way audio channel opens instantly, the speaker's ring pulsing green, no one having to answer.

interactions.title: Teach it a new trick with a Skill
interactions.description: Enable one of tens of thousands of third-party "Skills" and the speaker gains a new behavior on command — order a pizza, play a trivia game, hear a meditation — without any new hardware.

interactions.title: Listen for breaking glass while you're out
interactions.description: Say "Alexa, I'm leaving" and the speaker switches to a guard mode, listening for the sound of breaking glass or a smoke or CO alarm and pinging your phone with a short audio clip if it hears one.

interactions.title: Reward a kid for saying the magic word
interactions.description: In its kids mode, when a child asks "Alexa, please…" the assistant answers and adds "by the way, thanks for asking so nicely" — a politeness nudge Amazon added after parents worried that barking orders at a speaker was teaching rudeness.

interactions.title: A TV anchor's stray sentence orders dollhouses
interactions.description: Because the speaker wakes on its name from across a room, a news anchor saying "Alexa, order me a dollhouse" on air — and a commercial naming a product — set off shopping attempts in viewers' homes; orders normally need a spoken confirmation to go through.
interactions.source_label: Global News — Echo orders from a TV ad

interactions.title: An older person who lives alone just talks to it
interactions.description: Homebound seniors end up spending real time chatting with the speaker — asking for jokes, music, the weather — and in a study of adults 75+ the sheer time spent interacting with Alexa predicted measurable drops in loneliness.
interactions.source_label: NCBI — voice assistants and loneliness in older adults

interactions.title: A bedtime story told by the room
interactions.description: A child asks the speaker for a story at lights-out and gets one read aloud, hands-free in the dark — leaning on the same audio-first storytelling and read-aloud Skills the device already runs, with no screen to glow.
